risks.title: Signed contract copy not received
risks.description: Contract confirmed as signed at MOM-006 (11 Jun 2026) by Mr. Ghayyour. However, WSP has not yet received the physical/digital signed copy as of 13 Jun 2026.
risks.mitigation: Chase Danube urgently. Stage 1a proforma invoice (AED 950,000) issued 13 Jun 2026 — VAT cert received 12 Jun. Signed copy needed for WSP records and to formally close commercial exposure.
risks.notes: MAJOR PROGRESS: Contract signed (announced 11 Jun). Work-at-risk phase CLOSED. VAT cert received 12 Jun. Stage 1a invoice issued 13 Jun. Risk downgraded from High to Medium — only outstanding item is receipt of signed copy.

risks.title: GFA volatility & design desync
risks.description: Post-freeze footprint or unit mix tweaks reset transport models, utility calcs and ITC approval clocks.
risks.mitigation: Design Freeze 1 enforced (Brief V7, 24 Apr). Any GFA change triggers formal Impact Note with cost/programme implications. Return Brief V7 signed by client.
risks.notes: Option 3B (600,000 sqm GFA) formally selected 14 May. Design Freeze 2 to be set at CMP submission.

risks.title: Absence of Affection Plan — DMT workaround active
risks.description: Full Affection Plan cannot be issued until 100% land transfer from ADPG to Danube complete. 79 days open. DMT standard protocol mandates comprehensive title deed before opening a case.
risks.mitigation: NEW STRATEGY (agreed MOM-006, 11 Jun): (1) Schedule ADPG alignment meeting this week — obtain official facilitation letter confirming Danube master developer agreement. (2) Structure DMT submission showing global master plan but identifying Phase 1 as 10% land package (capturing at least one major external access point). (3) Submit formal DMT/ITC technical inquiry post ADPG sign-off. Danube targeting 10% title deeds/procurement by September 2026.
risks.notes: Workaround strategy agreed 11 Jun MOM. ADPG meeting is now critical path — must happen this week (w/c 15 Jun). WSP emphasized Abu Dhabi regulations are strictly enforced with minimal tolerance for deviations.

risks.title: Baseline data inaccuracy
risks.description: Designs depend on client-supplied topographic and utility surveys. Late-stage inaccuracies make civil engineering abortive.
risks.mitigation: Early QA of surveys. RFI.018 issued for new topo survey of updated plot boundaries following Option 3B rectangular plot confirmation. Survey confidence statement added to Stage 0.
risks.notes: Updated rectangular plot boundaries confirmed 14 May. New topo survey requested (RFI.018).

risks.title: DMT community facility mandates
risks.description: Residential-heavy masterplan vision may breach DMT mandatory community-facility (CF) quotas, triggering submission rejection.
risks.mitigation: Caveat included in Return Brief V7. Danube-signed transmittal documents WSP's assumptions on CF provision. Early DMT quota checks being pursued via Enquiry Meeting agenda.
risks.notes: DMT meeting agenda drafted to address land acquisition and CF quota confirmation simultaneously.

risks.title: Hidden utility easements
risks.description: HV / ADNOC lines may cross the plot with non-buildable buffer zones, reducing net saleable GFA below 600,000 sqm target.
risks.mitigation: Early utility corridor mapping requested. Constraints mapping included in PCMP Stage 0. Utility diversions explicitly excluded from WSP scope.
risks.notes: 26MW ADPG master developer utility allocation vs 90MW WSP calculated requirement — formal technical challenge issued.

risks.title: Data dependency — X-Architects
risks.description: Untimely receipt of CAD/GIS data (surrounding levels, road tie-ins, adjacent plot boundaries) from X-Architects — master developer's architect for the wider KEZAD Town Centre.
risks.mitigation: RFI.006 and RFI.018 issued. CAD data gaps flagged in Stage 1a submission. ADPG facilitation requested. Option 3B rectangular plot reduces dependency on X-Architects' curved alignment.
risks.notes: Option 3B rectangular plot (away from curved road) partially reduces this risk. Internal alignment call scheduled week of 18 May.

risks.title: Brief volatility
risks.description: Frequent client changes to unit mix, retail percentage, or GFA after Stage Gate 1a would trigger abortive work, extending programme and increasing cost.
risks.mitigation: Design Freeze 1 (Brief V7) enforced from 24 Apr. Changes post-freeze trigger formal Impact Note with commercial and programme implications. Re-baselined to Option 3B confirmed 14 May.
risks.notes: Option 3B (full GFA, hybrid density: villas/townhouses + G+5 gateway apartments) formally selected by client at executive meeting 14 May 2026.

risks.title: Adjacent interface coordination
risks.description: Changes to neighbouring development interfaces (wider KEZAD Town Centre by ADPG/X-Architects) could force boundary-zone design revisions post-freeze.
risks.mitigation: Boundaries mapped early and documented in Stage 0. Post-freeze changes triggered via formal Variation Order. RFI.016 issued requesting written confirmation on parallel sequencing.
risks.notes: Danube land acquisition of ~10% from ADPG reduces interface uncertainty. Full acquisition of remaining 90% expected.

risks.title: UK design consultant — regulatory non-compliance risk
risks.description: Incoming UK-based design consultant may not comprehend Abu Dhabi's rigid DMT/ITC regulatory framework. Historical precedent: Eagle Hills Ramhan Island — Perkins/BIG concepts faced severe DMT/ITC rejections resulting in lost villa yields and halted phases.
risks.mitigation: WSP to act as technical bridge between UK studio and local authorities. UK team limited to facade, styling, and interiors only — not permitted to alter road networks, plot boundaries, or engineering grids. WSP to host mandatory onboarding workshop immediately on contract.
risks.notes: Raised at MOM-PS-0010, 11 Jun 2026. UK consultant not yet contracted. Governance framework agreed. Onboarding workshop to be scheduled on contract execution.
